222	// countOccurrences is a small test helper; we avoid strings.Count import noise here.
223	func countOccurrences(s, sub string) int {
224		count := 0
225		start := 0
226		for {
227			idx := indexFrom(s, sub, start)
228			if idx == -1 {
229				return count
230			}
231			count++
232			start = idx + len(sub)
233		}
234	}
235	
236	func indexFrom(s, sub string, start int) int {
237		if start > len(s) {
238			return -1
239		}
240		rest := s[start:]
241		for i := 0; i+len(sub) <= len(rest); i++ {
242			if rest[i:i+len(sub)] == sub {
243				return start + i
244			}
245		}
246		return -1
247	}

10	# Feature: DDx Agent Skills
11	
12	**ID:** FEAT-011
13	**Status:** Implemented (root `ddx` skill with nested workflow skills present in `skills/`, `cli/internal/skills/`, `.agents/skills/`, `.claude/skills/`, and `.ddx/skills/`; site copy pending)
14	**Priority:** P1
15	**Owner:** DDx Team
16	
17	## Overview
18	
19	DDx ships a single root agent-facing skill (`ddx`) that makes any
20	skills-compatible coding agent "ddx-aware" after `ddx init`. Under that root,
21	DDx may ship nested workflow skills such as `bead-breakdown/`, `replay-bead/`,
22	`compare-prompts/`, and the bead-lifecycle quality skill governed by ADR-023.
23	The root skill and nested skills are written to the
24	[agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io) open standard so they work identically
25	in Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, OpenCode, and any other
26	harness that implements the standard.
27	
28	When the user says "do work", "review this", "what's on the queue",
29	"create a bead", or any DDx concept, the harness discovers the `ddx`
30	skill via its description, loads `SKILL.md`, and routes via an
31	explicit intent table into `reference/*.md` files that contain the
32	domain guidance.
33	
34	## Problem Statement
35	
36	Prior iterations of FEAT-011 shipped ~7 sibling skills
37	(`ddx-bead`, `ddx-run`, `ddx-review`, `ddx-status`,
38	`ddx-install`, `ddx-doctor`). Real-world usage exposed problems:
39	
40	- **Intent ambiguity.** Users say "do work", not "/ddx-run". A flat
41	  list of named slash commands forces the harness to guess between
42	  `/ddx-bead` vs `/ddx-run` vs `/ddx-work` from natural-language
43	  phrases.
44	- **Vocabulary drift.** Each skill redefined terms (bead, queue,
45	  harness, review) inline; wording diverged across files and from
46	  FEAT-* specs.
47	- **Workflow-opinion leakage.** `ddx-bead` mandated `helix` labels
48	  and documented `phase:*` labels — HELIX methodology opinions
49	  inside a core DDx skill.
50	- **Skill tree drift.** Two copies of most skills
51	  (`cli/internal/skills/` embedded vs top-level `/skills/`) with
52	  real content divergence.
53	- **Init gap.** `ddx init` copied only 2 of 7 embedded skills; the
54	  rest never surfaced to Claude Code unless the user re-installed
55	  manually.
56	- **Portability.** Skills used Claude-Code-only frontmatter fields
57	  (`argument-hint`) that are silently ignored by Codex and other
58	  harnesses, and reached for Claude-Code-only patterns
59	  (`context: fork`) that don't exist elsewhere.
60	
61	The consolidated design fixes each of these.
62	
63	## Architecture
64	
65	### Root skill plus nested workflow skills
66	
67	```
68	skills/ddx/
69	├── SKILL.md                # overview, vocabulary, intent-router directive
70	├── reference/
71	│   ├── beads.md            # writing execution-ready beads (best practices)
72	│   ├── work.md             # draining the queue, execute-bead, verify + close
73	│   ├── review.md           # bead-review (AC grade) + quorum code review
74	│   ├── agents.md           # harness/profile dispatch, personas
75	│   ├── interactive.md      # default queue-steward conversation workflow
76	│   └── status.md           # queue state, doctor, health checks
77	├── evals/
78	│   └── routing.jsonl       # phrase→mode/reference/action fixtures
79	├── bead-breakdown/
80	│   └── SKILL.md            # workflow skill composition over ddx bead create
81	├── replay-bead/
82	│   └── SKILL.md            # workflow skill composition over ddx run / try
83	└── ...
84	```
85

88	intent router matches a phrase, the harness reads the matching

100	bead readiness assessment, lint/rubric scoring, and post-attempt triage. The

136	### Interactive-steward routing
137	
138	`reference/interactive.md` is the first stop for broad conversational DDx
139	prompts that combine queue orientation, planning, review, guidance folding,
140	spec alignment, and bead breakdown. It delegates to `status.md`, `review.md`,
141	`beads.md`, and `work.md` instead of duplicating their domain guidance.
142	
143	Router precedence is load-bearing:
144	
145	1. Explicit worker commands such as `ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead
146	   `<id>`", or "start the worker" route to `bead_execution` and `work.md`.
147	2. Broad prompts such as "what's blocking the queue", "review with fresh
148	   eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "make this testable", and
149	   "break this down into specs and beads" route to `interactive-steward` and
150	   `interactive.md`.
151	3. Explicit code/doc edit requests such as "fix this bug" or "update this
152	   file" route to `direct_user_implementation`.
153	4. Explicit review-only requests route to `review` and remain read-only unless
154	   the user asks for fixes.
155	
156	`interactive.md` defines phase output contracts:
157	
158	- `orient` returns `QueueFacts`. It probes `ddx work focus --help`; if the
159	  command is unavailable, it falls back to `ddx bead status`, `ddx bead blocked`,
160	  `ddx bead ready`, and targeted `ddx bead show`. If `ddx work focus` exists
161	  but fails, the steward reports that failure.
162	- `plan` returns `SessionBrief`.
163	- `fresh-eyes review` returns `Findings` and `Verdict`. Multi-harness
164	  adversarial review is consent-gated unless explicitly requested.
165	- `fold guidance` returns `Accepted`, `Rejected`, `Unresolved`, and a revised
166	  plan.
167	- `align specs` returns `SpecDeltas`.
168	- `breakdown` returns filed bead IDs, parent/dependency edges, named tests, and
169	  verification commands.
170	
171	The route fixtures under `skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl` assert a stable row
172	schema: `phrase`, `mode`, `references`, `queue_commands`,
173	`tracker_mutation_allowed`, `code_edits_allowed`, and
174	`expected_next_action`. The evaluation driver consumes those fields directly;
175	it must not keep validating only the legacy `expected_reference` /
176	`expected_cli` pair after the richer schema lands. Negative fixtures are
177	required: "what should I work on next" routes to `interactive-steward` with no
178	code edits, whereas "implement the top ready bead" is direct implementation only
179	because the implementation verb is explicit.
180	
181	### Nested workflow skills
182	
183	Subagent orchestration remains harness-specific (Claude Code has
184	`.claude/agents/` + `context: fork`; Codex has its own subagent surface;
185	others differ). DDx does not ship harness-specific subagent definitions.
186	
187	DDx does ship nested workflow skills under the root skill tree when a
188	workflow has enough procedure to deserve its own reusable instructions. Current
189	examples include `bead-breakdown/`, `replay-bead/`, `compare-prompts/`,
190	`benchmark-suite/`, `effort-estimate/`, and `adversarial-review/`. ADR-023
191	(`../../02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md`) adds the same
192	model for bead-lifecycle quality: bead readiness assessment is the canonical
193	pre-claim decision, lint/rubric scoring is the rubric pass inside readiness,
194	and post-attempt triage is the after-evidence classification. Those steps
195	invoke a nested workflow skill while keeping routing through the root `ddx`
196	skill.
197	
198	Nested workflow skills are compositions over FEAT-010's three layers. They may
199	tell the harness to run `ddx run`, `ddx try`, or `ddx work`, but they do not
200	create a fourth run layer, bespoke storage shape, or harness-specific
201	frontmatter contract.
202	
203	The bead-lifecycle skill owns bead readiness assessment, lint/rubric scoring,
204	post-attempt triage, and refine guidance for this policy surface. Those
205	responsibilities are separate from the legacy `MODE: intake` compatibility
206	wording, which remains only as an implementation detail for older hooks.
207	
208	### Installation
209	
210	> See FEAT-015 (2026-04-29 amendment) for the authoritative install
211	> model: project-local copy semantics, zero symlinks created by
212	> `ddx install`, and the no-`~` manifest invariant.
213	
214	- `ddx init` copies `skills/ddx/` into `.claude/skills/ddx/`,
215	  `.agents/skills/ddx/`, and `.ddx/skills/ddx/` as real files
216	  (symlinks break after `git clone` on a fresh machine, and DDx no
217	  longer creates symlinks during install — see FEAT-015).
218	- DDx maintains five shipped copies of the root skill tree:
219	  `skills/ddx/` as source, `cli/internal/skills/ddx/` for `go:embed`,

1	# Interactive Steward — Default Queue-Steward Conversation Workflow
2	
3	This file governs broad conversational DDx sessions: queue orientation, planning,
4	review, guidance folding, spec alignment, and bead breakdown. It is the first
5	stop for any DDx request that is not an explicit worker command, direct code
6	edit, or explicit review-only request.
7	
8	## Mode
9	
10	**Mode: `interactive-steward`**
11	
12	The interactive steward combines orientation, planning, review, alignment, and
13	breakdown into a coherent conversation loop. It does **not** implement ready
14	beads directly — that is `bead_execution` mode (`ddx work` / `ddx try`).
15	
16	When a human says "what should I work on next", "what's blocking the queue",
17	"review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "break this down into specs
18	and beads", or "make this testable", they are talking to the steward, not the
19	worker.
20	
21	## Router precedence
22	
23	1. **Explicit worker commands** — `ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead
24	   `<id>`", "start the worker" → `bead_execution` → `reference/work.md`. Stop;
25	   do not enter steward mode.
26	2. **Broad conversational DDx prompts** — queue orientation, planning, review,
27	   guidance folding, spec alignment, breakdown → **`interactive-steward`** → this
28	   file, then delegate to the matching domain reference.
29	3. **Explicit code/doc edits** — "fix this bug", "update this file" →
30	   `direct_user_implementation`.
31	4. **Explicit review-only requests** — "review this", "check against spec" →
32	   `review` → `reference/review.md`.
33	
34	The steward delegates to domain references rather than duplicating their guidance:
35	
36	- Queue/health queries → `reference/status.md`
37	- Review → `reference/review.md`
38	- Bead writing/breakdown → `reference/beads.md`
39	- Queue execution → `reference/work.md`
40	
41	## Session brief
42	
43	At the start of an interactive session, the steward collects a `SessionBrief`:
44	
45	- **Focus**: the active bead ID or goal, if stated.
46	- **Horizon**: how much work the user wants to get through this session.
47	- **Constraints**: blockers, frozen areas, external dependencies.
48	- **Mode preference**: plan, review, align specs, or break down work.
49	
50	The `SessionBrief` is kept in-conversation and referenced for all follow-on
51	actions. **Never write the brief to `/tmp` or a path outside the repository** —
52	if it needs persistence, embed it into a bead description or spec update.
53	
54	## Phase outputs
55	
56	Each steward phase produces a named output contract. Bead/spec outputs inline
57	the accepted session brief; they do not rely on chat history.
58	
59	### orient → QueueFacts
60	
61	Probes queue state. First try:
62	
63	```bash
64	ddx work focus --help
65	```
66	
67	If the command is available, run:
68	
69	```bash
70	ddx work focus
71	```
72	
73	If `ddx work focus` is unavailable (exits non-zero for `--help`), fall back to:
74	
75	```bash
76	ddx bead status         # lifecycle and derived queue counts
77	ddx bead blocked        # status=blocked external/recheckable blockers
78	ddx bead ready          # actionable execution-ready open beads
79	ddx bead show <id>      # targeted inspection of the top ready bead
80	```
81	
82	If `ddx work focus` exists but fails, report that failure — do not silently fall
83	back.
84	
85	`QueueFacts` includes: total open, execution-ready bead IDs, dependency-waiting
86	beads, proposed/operator-attention beads, external blockers, and any error from
87	the focus command.
88	
89	### plan → SessionBrief
90	
91	Given `QueueFacts`, produce a `SessionBrief` for this session:
92	
93	- Which beads to attempt (ordered by priority + dependency satisfaction).
94	- Which actions are consent-gated (adversarial review, tracker mutations).
95	- What success looks like for the session.
96	
97	### fresh-eyes review → Findings + Verdict
98	
99	Structured local review of current branch or work-in-progress against bead ACs
100	or governing spec.
101	
102	Returns:
103	
104	- `Findings`: per-AC or per-concern items with file:line evidence.
105	- `Verdict`: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | BLOCK.
106	
107	**Multi-harness adversarial review is consent-gated.** Suggest it if the change
108	is high-stakes, but do not dispatch it unless the user explicitly agrees. Phrase
109	the suggestion as:
110	
111	> "Would you like me to run an adversarial multi-harness review? This dispatches
112	> `ddx run` across multiple personas and costs more time."
113	
114	### fold guidance → Accepted + Rejected + Unresolved + revised plan
115	
116	The user provides guidance (feedback, spec change, constraint) to fold into the
117	current plan:
118	
119	1. Parse each guidance item.
120	2. Accept items that align with the session brief and spec.
121	3. Reject items that conflict with governing artifacts (state the conflict).
122	4. Mark unresolved items that need a spec update or human decision.
123	5. Produce a revised `SessionBrief` incorporating the accepted guidance.
124	
125	### align specs → SpecDeltas
126	
127	Compare current implementation or bead AC against the governing spec (`FEAT-*`,
128	`ADR-*`, `SD-*`):
129	
130	1. Read the governing artifact (follow `spec-id` if present on the bead).
131	2. Identify divergences between spec requirements and current AC or code.
132	3. Return `SpecDeltas`: which spec sections are satisfied, which are diverged,
133	   and what changes are needed to align.
134	
135	If no `spec-id` is present, ask the user which governing artifact to align
136	against before proceeding.
137	
138	### breakdown → filed bead IDs + dependency edges + named tests + verification commands
139	
140	Break an epic or broad goal into execution-ready beads:
141	
142	1. Produce a bead breakdown with title, description, and AC for each slice.
143	2. Each AC names at least one test function or `go test -run` filter.
144	3. File each bead via `ddx bead create`.
145	4. Wire dependency edges via `ddx bead dep add`.
146	5. Return: filed bead IDs, parent/dependency graph, named tests per bead, and
147	   verification commands.
148	
149	Durable bead outputs inline the accepted `SessionBrief` and do not reference
150	`/tmp` files or chat history.
151	
152	## Mutation policy
153	
154	| Phase | Tracker mutations | Code edits |
155	|---|---|---|
156	| orient | no | no |
157	| plan | no | no |
158	| fresh-eyes review | no | no |
159	| fold guidance | no (unless breakdown follows) | no |
160	| align specs | no | no |
161	| breakdown | yes (`ddx bead create` / `ddx bead dep add`) | no |
162	
163	The steward **never** edits code directly. It plans, reviews, aligns, and breaks
164	down. Actual code changes happen in `bead_execution` mode via `ddx work` /
165	`ddx try`.
166	
167	## Queue orientation fallback
168	
169	If `ddx work focus` is not yet available, use the three-command fallback:
170	
171	```bash
172	ddx bead status
173	ddx bead blocked
174	ddx bead ready
175	```
176	
177	Report the three outputs as `QueueFacts`. Note that `ddx work focus` is not yet
178	available so the user knows the fallback was used.
179	
180	## Spec alignment
181	
182	To align specs, read the governing artifact referenced by `spec-id` on the
183	current bead or task context. If no `spec-id` is present, ask the user which
184	governing artifact to align against before proceeding.
185	
186	## Breakdown
187	
188	A well-formed breakdown satisfies the 8-criterion bead-authoring rubric (see
189	`docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md`). Each child bead must be
190	self-contained: a competent agent given only the bead body must be able to pick
191	a file to edit and run tests without asking.
192	
193	Breakdown outputs include:
194	
195	- Filed bead IDs (from `ddx bead create` output).
196	- Parent/dependency edges (from `ddx bead dep add` output).
197	- Named test functions or `go test -run` filters per bead.
198	- Verification commands per bead.
199

# DDx

This project uses [DDx](https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx) for
document-driven development. Use the ` + "`" + `ddx` + "`" + ` skill for beads, work,
review, agents, and status — every skills-compatible harness (Claude

` + "`" + `bead_execution` + "`" + `. Explicit code/doc edit requests route to
` + "`" + `direct_user_implementation` + "`" + `. Explicit review-only requests route to
` + "`" + `review` + "`" + `.

` + "`" + `DDX_MODE=bead_execution` + "`" + ` overrides only the interactive queue-steward default.
It **never** overrides tracker, merge, commit, safety, or verification policy —

Committed. All ACs satisfied:

- **AC1**: `ddxAgentsBlock()` now has a "## Default Interactive Mode" section naming `interactive-steward`/`queue_steward`, `bead_execution`, `direct_user_implementation`, and `review`.
- **AC2**: Block states `DDX_MODE=bead_execution` overrides only the interactive queue-steward default, never tracker/merge/commit/safety/verification.
- **AC3**: Block states read/plan/review/fold are non-mutating; tracker mutation requires explicit durable-output verbs; code edits require explicit implementation intent or `bead_execution`.
- **AC4**: `TestGenerateAgentsMD_IncludesInteractiveStewardGuidance` added and passes.
- **AC5-8**: All four existing tests pass, full `./cmd/...` suite passes, lefthook pre-commit passes.
